coachLine: Sim-optimal for 10s rotation vs squishies: first spike lands around minute 12 (Spectral Schematics), third item by minute 24. Take Demiurge: +19.5% on 20-second fights at minute 15.

all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

metaBuilds.title: Magical Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 24 — about 892 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +23% more one-combo burst: try Spectral Schematics over Entropy (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Magical Brawler
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for extended fights vs bruisers, online around minute 24 — about 1,338 damage over a 20s fight in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +77% more extended fights vs bruisers: try Spectral Schematics over Tainted Scepter (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Magical Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 25 — about 3,832 effective HP vs physical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +36% more physical survivability: try Spectral Schematics over Entropy (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. The Fey deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Argus deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Countess deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 27% of its bite; you give up 24% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Gideon deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 32% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your window is early: force trades before their core completes, and do not coin-flip fights past the crossover. Iggy & Scorch deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your window is early: force trades before their core completes, and do not coin-flip fights past the crossover. Lt. Belica executes low targets with Neural Disruptor — don't linger below half HP.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Wraith Leggings: their 3s all-in loses 30% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Zinx's main threat is Ricochet; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.
Even kill threat throughout: this lane is decided by mechanics and wave control, not scaling. Phase's main threat is Energy Lance; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.
They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Dekker deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Riktor ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.
You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Adele deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Unbroken Will: their 3s all-in loses 27% of its bite; you give up 0% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Mourn's main threat is Abduct; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.
